name: Red Wine Sauce- Great Chefs
recipeInstructions:
1. In a sauté pan, heat the olive oil.
2. Add the salmon bones to the pan and sauté for about 1 minute.
3. Add butter (about 2 tablespoons), 1 cup mirepoix, 2 bay leaves, 1/4 teaspoon thyme, 1/4 teaspoon white peppercorns, and 2 tablespoons shallot puree.
4. Add cognac and flame.
5. Deglaze with 1 cup red wine and cook over high heat for 5 to 10 minutes.
6. Meanwhile, in a second sauté pan, melt 2 tablespoons butter.
7. Add 2 tablespoons shallot puree, 1 cup mirepoix, 2 bay leaves, 1/4 teaspoon white peppercorns, 1/4 teaspoon oregano, 1/4 teaspoon thyme, and 3 cups red wine.
8. Reduce over medium heat to dry.
9. Add 1 cup fish stock to the sauté pan with salmon bones. Cook about 5 minutes.
10. Deglaze the reduction (shallot-red wine) in the second sauté pan with about 3/4 of the strained liquid from the first sauté pan (salmon bones and fish stock).
11. Reduce ingredients in the saucepan by two-thirds (not dry).
12. Add remainder of butter, whisk, and add salt and pepper to taste.
13. Strain and reserve.
